title: Where the grain goes
callout:
Width of each line is that
state’s share of grain exports.
steps: Three states grow most of the grain that leaves through the Gulf. Here they are as a simple chart — each bar as wide as that state’s share.
Now watch where that grain actually comes from. The bars lift off and settle onto the map, at each state’s place.
The routes converge and pour south, merging into a single trunk bound for the export terminals on the Gulf coast.
The width never lies: the trunk is exactly as thick as the three tributaries combined.
